web.title: Managing your membership in organizations - GitHub Docs
web.description: Viewing people's roles in an organization. You can view a list of the people in your organization and filter by their role. Requesting organization approval ...
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership#main-content)

# Managing your membership in organizations

If you're a member of an organization, you can publicize or hide your membership, view other people's roles, and remove yourself from the organization.

## [Accessing an organization](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/accessing-an-organization)

To access an organization that you're a member of, you must sign in to your personal account.

## [Viewing people's roles in an organization](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/viewing-peoples-roles-in-an-organization)

You can view a list of the people in your organization and filter by their role.

## [Requesting organization approval for OAuth apps](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/requesting-organization-approval-for-oauth-apps)

You can request approval for OAuth apps to access organization resources.

## [Publicizing or hiding organization membership](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/publicizing-or-hiding-organization-membership)

Show avatars for organizations where you're an organization member.

## [Removing yourself from an organization](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/removing-yourself-from-an-organization)

If you're an outside collaborator or a member of an organization, you can leave the organization at any time.

## [Removing yourself from an enterprise](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/removing-yourself-from-an-enterprise)

You can leave an enterprise after removing yourself from every organization in the enterprise.

web.title: (GitHub Enterprise Cloud) Search across all organizations in an ...
web.description: The GitHub Code Search capability only allows the following scopes. Is it possible to scope the search to all organizations (only) in my enterprise?

Question
### Body
I work for a large enterprise & a GitHub Enterprise user. There are 100s of GitHub Organizations in this enterprise setup.
The GitHub Code Search capability only allows the following scopes
- within a repo
- within an organization
- all GitHub
Is it possible to scope the search to all organizations (only) in my enterprise? | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) 130You must be logged in to vote All reactions - ![heart](https://github.githubassets.com/assets/2764-982dc91ea48a.png)38 Answered by [queenofcorgis](https://github.com/queenofcorgis)[on Nov 5, 2025Nov 5, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#discussioncomment-14882483) Thank you everyone for your patience and the feedback which helped us ship this feature, you can now search across Enterprises, we [updated our docs to include this](https://docs.github.com/en/enterprise-cloud@latest/search-github/github-code-search/understanding-github-code-search-syntax#enterprise-qualifier), stay tuned for the changelog! [View full answer](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#discussioncomment-14882483) ## Replies: 17 comments · 30 replies Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply ### [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4266541?s=64&v=4)\ JoshuaTheMiller](https://github.com/JoshuaTheMiller) [on Apr 3, 2023Apr 3, 2023](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-5510646) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | As of now and as far as I'm aware, **no- it is not possible** 😟 With that being said, hopefully more folks come across this Discussion and Vote on it so that GitHub knows that it is a highly useful feature (one that is _probably_ highly requested as well!).
In case folks say otherwise- yes, we could create a massive search-string that includes a list of all Enterprise Orgs as Org filters. With large Enterprises though, this gets unwieldy and we **will** bump into search size limits. | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) All reactions Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply ### [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/16908880?s=64&v=4)\ MartinLesterSynamedia](https://github.com/MartinLesterSynamedia) [on Jul 29, 2024Jul 29, 2024](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-10179625) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | You can get this data using the GraphQL API
```
query get_orgs($enterprise:String!, $pos:String) {
enterprise(slug: $enterprise) {
organizations(first: 100, after: $pos) {
edges{
node {
login
}
}
pageInfo {
hasNextPage
endCursor
}
}
}
}
```
```
{
"enterprise": "ENTERPRISE-NAME",
"pos": ""
}
```
Try it with the [GraphQL explorer](https://docs.github.com/en/graphql/overview/explorer)
When using pagination, you need to keep calling the query with `pos = endCursor` till `hasNextPage` is false. | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) 1You must be logged in to vote All reactions - ![-1](https://github.githubassets.com/assets/1f44e-ce91733aae25.png)1 1 reply [![@eisenhowerj](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3538462?s=60&v=4)](https://github.com/eisenhowerj) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply #### [eisenhowerj](https://github.com/eisenhowerj) [on Sep 3, 2024Sep 3, 2024](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-10533866) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | I think you misunderstood the request. We don't need a list of all organizations within an enterprise account; we need the ability to _search_ across all orgs e.g. this [https://docs.github.com/en/graphql/reference/queries#search](https://docs.github.com/en/graphql/reference/queries#search) | BetaWas this translation helpful? [https://github.com/newsroom/press-releases/data-residency-in-the-eu](https://github.com/newsroom/press-releases/data-residency-in-the-eu) | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) 1You must be logged in to vote All reactions 2 replies [![@vegardx](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/510694?s=60&v=4)](https://github.com/vegardx) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply #### [vegardx](https://github.com/vegardx) [on Oct 8, 2024Oct 8, 2024](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-10878254) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | Sort of, but it only partially solves the issue. There's no way to do enterprise-wide searches from the search within an organization, this can only be done from the enterprise landing page or a separate search page within your tenant, even though the UX for the search is identical, the functionality is widely different. And from a users perspective this makes no sense. How you'd effectively communicate this to your enterprise users I struggle to see.
My guess would be that GitHub does something similar to how GitHub Enterprise Cloud with Data Residency for GitHub Enterprise Cloud in general. I never understood why you'd even want to have GitHub Enterprise Managed Users on github.com, it just confuses people, gives you headaches with people not being able to use the same SSH key and whatnot. | BetaWas this translation helpful? >
> Question
>
> ### Body
>
> I work for a large enterprise & a GitHub Enterprise user. There are 100s of GitHub Organizations in this enterprise setup.
>
> The GitHub Code Search capability only allows the following scopes
>
> - within a repo
> - within an organization
> - all GitHub
>
> Is it possible to scope the search to all organizations (only) in my enterprise? | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) 3You must be logged in to vote All reactions 1 reply [![@Bashirsaba](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/189290487?s=60&v=4)](https://github.com/Bashirsaba) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply #### [Bashirsaba](https://github.com/Bashirsaba) [on Oct 17, 2025Oct 17, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-14710294) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | Ok sir | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) All reactions Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply ### [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/92824518?s=64&v=4)\ queenofcorgis](https://github.com/queenofcorgis) [on Nov 5, 2025Nov 5, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-14882483) Maintainer Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | Thank you everyone for your patience and the feedback which helped us ship this feature, you can now search across Enterprises, we [updated our docs to include this](https://docs.github.com/en/enterprise-cloud@latest/search-github/github-code-search/understanding-github-code-search-syntax#enterprise-qualifier), stay tuned for the changelog! | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) Marked as answer 3You must be logged in to vote All reactions - ![tada](https://github.githubassets.com/assets/1f389-36899a2cb781.png)8 5 replies [![@WyriHaximus](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147145?s=60&v=4)](https://github.com/WyriHaximus) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply #### [WyriHaximus](https://github.com/WyriHaximus) [on Nov 6, 2025Nov 6, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-14889886) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | [@queenofcorgis](https://github.com/queenofcorgis) Awesome, really really happy to see this feature land ❤️ ! One note tho, if your enterprise has an understore in it say `my_enterprise` you need to use `enterprise:myenterprise` instead of `enterprise:my_enterprise` to make it work. | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) All reactions [![@jorendorff](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/283361?s=60&v=4)](https://github.com/jorendorff) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply #### [jorendorff](https://github.com/jorendorff) [on Nov 6, 2025Nov 6, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-14894997) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | Thanks for pointing this out.
For anyone having trouble figuring out what enterprise ID to use: Go to any repository in your enterprise and click in the search bar or press `/`. You'll see some search suggestions, like this:
![search suggestions](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/283361/510942018-24f56e8f-ae2b-4374-9e2f-5bef9c54d184.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTUiLCJleHAiOjE3NzQ3OTY1NDksIm5iZiI6MTc3NDc5NjI0OSwicGF0aCI6Ii8yODMzNjEvNTEwOTQyMDE4LTI0ZjU2ZThmLWFlMmItNDM3NC05ZTJmLTViZWY5YzU0ZDE4NC5wbmc_.eg3RWqdWPzRWBOzTSI-p6Ec3AJKMIneCv8UK2WCqu9A)
One of them will show the `enterprise:` syntax to use for your enterprise. | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) All reactions [![@WyriHaximus](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147145?s=60&v=4)](https://github.com/WyriHaximus) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply #### [WyriHaximus](https://github.com/WyriHaximus) [on Nov 6, 2025Nov 6, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-14895217) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | Correct, that is how we found and realised this. The other thing that caught us off guard a bit is that this is only for code search, looking at this topic that makes sense 😅 . | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) All reactions [![@gjasny](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1165598?s=60&v=4)](https://github.com/gjasny) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply #### [gjasny](https://github.com/gjasny) [on Nov 23, 2025Nov 23, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-15052723) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | Thanks for implementing that. Could you please give an example how to use the `enterprise:myenterprise` in a `gh search code` or `gh api` command call? | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) All reactions [![@look](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10186?s=60&v=4)](https://github.com/look) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply #### [look](https://github.com/look) [on Nov 25, 2025Nov 25, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-15079482) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | Unfortunately, this is not available in the API at this time, so using `gh` will not work. | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) All reactions Answer selected by [queenofcorgis](https://github.com/queenofcorgis) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply ### [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/162571804?s=64&v=4)\ pedramalizade](https://github.com/pedramalizade) [on Dec 2, 2025Dec 2, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-15138144) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | You can’t scope GitHub Code Search to “all organizations in your Enterprise only.”
Currently, the available scopes are:
repo: specific repositories
org: a single organization
global: everything your account has access to (all orgs + all repos)
GitHub Enterprise doesn’t provide an “enterprise-level only” search filter.
If you want to limit results to your Enterprise organizations, the closest workaround is:
Run a global search
Add org:ORG-NAME filters for the orgs you specifically want
(Optional) Create a custom list of orgs and reuse that search query
It’s not perfect, but right now GitHub doesn’t expose a direct “enterprise scope” for Code Search.
If GitHub adds an Enterprise-wide search scope in the future, it should appear in the official Code Search docs. | BetaWas this translation helpful? Glad to see the enterprise: qualifier is now available—it will make Enterprise-wide searches much easier. | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510#) All reactions Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply ### [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/193112219?s=64&v=4)\ CodeWithMehru](https://github.com/CodeWithMehru) [on Dec 9, 2025Dec 9, 2025](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/49510\#discussioncomment-15206816) - | | | --- | | \*\*You can now search across all organizations in an enterprise using the new enterprise qualifier in GitHub Code Search. Use it like this for example
enterprise:your-enterprise-name
This will return results from all organizations that belong to that enterprise and that you have access to. The qualifier is generally available to GitHub Enterprise Cloud customers and behaves like other search qualifiers so it keeps the same limits and permission rules.
If you do not have the qualifier yet the workaround is to run a global search and add multiple org: qualifiers or use an external code search tool like Sourcegraph that can index many organizations.\*\* | BetaWas this translation helpful? web.title: Viewing people's roles in an organization - GitHub Docs
web.description: To filter the list by role, select the Role dropdown menu and click a role. Screenshot of the list of organization members. In the header of the list, a. If ...
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/viewing-peoples-roles-in-an-organization#main-content)

# Viewing people's roles in an organization

You can view a list of the people in your organization and filter by their role.

## Who can use this feature?

Organization members

Copy as Markdown

1. In the upper-right corner of GitHub, click your profile picture, then click **Organizations**.

2. Click the name of your organization.

3. Under your organization name, click **People**.



![Screenshot of the horizontal navigation bar for an organization. A tab, labeled with a person icon and "People," is outlined in dark orange.](https://docs.github.com/assets/cb-18976/images/help/organizations/organization-people-tab.png)

4. To filter the list by role, select the **Role** dropdown menu and click a role.



![Screenshot of the list of organization members. In the header of the list, a dropdown menu, labeled "Role," is outlined in dark orange.](https://docs.github.com/assets/cb-34919/images/help/organizations/view-list-of-people-in-org-by-role.png)


If your organization uses GitHub Enterprise Cloud, you can also view the enterprise owners who manage billing settings and policies for all your enterprise's organizations. For more information, see [the GitHub Enterprise Cloud documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/enterprise-cloud@latest/account-and-profile/setting-up-and-managing-your-personal-account-on-github/managing-your-membership-in-organizations/viewing-peoples-roles-in-an-organization#view-enterprise-owners-and-their-roles-in-an-organization).

## [Next steps](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/organization-membership/viewing-peoples-roles-in-an-organization\#next-steps)

To learn more about organization roles, see [Roles in an organization](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-peoples-access-to-your-organization-with-roles/roles-in-an-organization).

web.title: Filtering repositories in your organization using the repository table
web.description: Filtering the repository table with the search bar. In the upper-right corner of GitHub, click your profile picture, then click Organizations.
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/how-tos/secure-at-scale/configure-organization-security/manage-your-coverage/filtering-repositories-in-your-organization-using-the-repository-table#main-content)

# Filtering repositories in your organization using the repository table

You can filter the repository table for your organization to better manage the security settings of specific repositories.

## Who can use this feature?

Organization owners, security managers, and organization members with the **admin** role

Copy as Markdown

## In this article

You can filter the repository table in your organization to quickly find and manage specific repositories when applying security configurations or managing Advanced Security license usage in the organization. This article explains how to use filters in the repository table.

## [Filtering the repository table with the search bar](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/how-tos/secure-at-scale/configure-organization-security/manage-your-coverage/filtering-repositories-in-your-organization-using-the-repository-table\#filtering-the-repository-table-with-the-search-bar)

1. In the upper-right corner of GitHub, click your profile picture, then click **Organizations**.

2. Under your organization name, click **Settings**. If you cannot see the "Settings" tab, select the  dropdown menu, then click **Settings**.



![Screenshot of the tabs in an organization's profile. The "Settings" tab is outlined in dark orange.](https://docs.github.com/assets/cb-49309/images/help/discussions/org-settings-global-nav-update.png)

3. In the "Security" section of the sidebar, select the **Advanced Security** dropdown menu, then click **Repositories**.

4. In the "Apply configurations" section, use the **Search repositories** search bar to filter repositories in your organization in one of two ways:
   - Find repositories by name with free-text search
   - Select filters from the dropdown menu that appears once you click the search bar

## [Filtering the repository table with the advanced filter builder](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/how-tos/secure-at-scale/configure-organization-security/manage-your-coverage/filtering-repositories-in-your-organization-using-the-repository-table\#filtering-the-repository-table-with-the-advanced-filter-builder)

1. In the upper-right corner of GitHub, click your profile picture, then click **Organizations**.

2. Under your organization name, click **Settings**. If you cannot see the "Settings" tab, select the  dropdown menu, then click **Settings**.



![Screenshot of the tabs in an organization's profile. The "Settings" tab is outlined in dark orange.](https://docs.github.com/assets/cb-49309/images/help/discussions/org-settings-global-nav-update.png)

3. In the "Security" section of the sidebar, select the **Advanced Security** dropdown menu, then click **Repositories**.

4. To open the advanced filter dialog, in the "Apply configurations" section, click **Filter**.

5. In the "Advanced filters" window, select the **Add a filter** dropdown menu, then click a filter.

6. To search for repositories matching the selected filter, fill out the available fields for that filter, then click **Apply**. You can repeat this process to add as many filters as you would like to your search.

7. Optionally, to remove a filter from your search, click **Filter**. In the row of the filter you want to remove, click , then click **Apply**.

web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-membership-in-your-organization#main-content)

# Managing membership in your organization

After you create your organization, you can invite people to become members of the organization. You can also remove members of the organization, and reinstate former members.

- [Inviting users to join your organization](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-membership-in-your-organization/inviting-users-to-join-your-organization)
- [Canceling or editing an invitation to join your organization](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-membership-in-your-organization/canceling-or-editing-an-invitation-to-join-your-organization)
- [Removing a member from your organization](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-membership-in-your-organization/removing-a-member-from-your-organization)
- [Reinstating a former member of your organization](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-membership-in-your-organization/reinstating-a-former-member-of-your-organization)
- [Exporting member information for your organization](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-membership-in-your-organization/exporting-member-information-for-your-organization)
- [Can I create accounts for people in my organization?](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-membership-in-your-organization/can-i-create-accounts-for-people-in-my-organization)
